chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No. 1889 of 2025 [Akbar Ali & another vs. The State] Cr. Bail Application No. 876 of 2025 [Khurram Kambaiz Shaikh vs. The State] Applicants: through M/s. Umar Farooq and Mallag Assa Dashti, Advocates. Respondent: The State through Mr. Sarfraz Ahmed Mangi, Special Prosecutor ANF. Date of hearing: 16.09.2025 Date of Order: 23.09.2025 ORDER At the very outset, learned Special Prosecutor ANF has informed the Court that the trial is at the verge of conclusion as all the prosecution witnesses have been examined. Learned counsels for the applicants also confirm the same. Relying on the principle laid down by the Hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan reported in 2002 SCMR 1381 (Muhammad Nawaz vs. The State) and 2011 SCMR 1332 (Rehmatullah vs. The State) it will not be proper to either allow or dismiss the bail application as any order of this Court may impact the case of either party. Learned trial Court is directed to conclude the trial within a period of eight weeks and report compliance through MIT- II. JUDGE Saleem/P.S.
